title: Olytic Plugin Identity
description: Canonical schema for the fields that make a plugin uniquely identifiable in the Olytic ecosystem. These fields appear in plugin.json, workspace.json, and the marketplace registry. Used for dependency validation and workspace compatibility checks.
read_timing: deploy time — validated when plugin is installed or workspace.json is loaded

name.description: Kebab-case plugin name — unique across the Olytic plugin catalog

validation_rules: name must be unique across the workspace — checked against workspace.json on install
plugin.json must NOT contain a connectors key — the Claude validator requires connector URLs which org-installed MCP servers cannot provide at build time
version must increment on every release — downgrades are rejected
notes: plugin-identity-schema validates the plugin.json manifest fields
Valid plugin.json keys: name, version, description, author, keywords, hooks — all others cause upload failure
Connector/MCP server availability is documented in skill and agent instructions, not in plugin.json
